name: nav-app-chrome
role: Top-level scope for an in-app shell header. Polymorphic <header role='banner'> always-sticky, with three rows of optional content: workspace switcher + breadcrumb + action cluster + user menu (main row); optional page-tabs row (secondary nav within the current page); optional offline / status banner (when connection drops). Three variants — standard (full set), with-subnav (main + page-tabs), condensed (no breadcrumb, minimal action cluster, used in fullscreen-editor modes).

semanticStructure: <header role='banner'> — page banner landmark; always-sticky at top: 0
<nav aria-label='App shell'> — main nav landmark with workspace switcher + breadcrumb + actions
(optional) <nav aria-label='Page tabs'> — page-level secondary nav row

variants.standard: Full main row. Used on most workspace pages, project pages, document lists.
variants.with-subnav: Main row + page-tabs subnav row. Used on document pages, project pages, settings (where page-tabs surface 'Overview / Activity / Settings').
variants.condensed: Main row only with no breadcrumb. Workspace switcher + minimal action cluster + user menu. Used in fullscreen-editor modes (e.g. focus-mode in a notes editor) where the chrome should recede.

states.search-open: Cmd+K palette is open. Body scroll locked. nav-app-chrome retains visual state but its global-search trigger gets [aria-expanded='true'].
states.menu-open: Any popover menu (workspace switcher, user menu, notifications) is open. nav-app-chrome stays put; the trigger element carries [aria-expanded='true'].
states.offline: Connection lost. <app-status-banner> renders at the top with --critical tone. All sync-dependent actions become loading or disabled.

ariaPatterns.appNavLabel: <nav aria-label='App shell'> distinguishes from any in-page <nav>.
ariaPatterns.stickyBehaviour: position: sticky; top: 0. Border-bottom and z-index ensure it sits above scrolled content.
ariaPatterns.globalShortcutKeyboard: Cmd+K (Mac) / Ctrl+K (Windows) opens the global search. The trigger has aria-keyshortcuts='Meta+K' to advertise the binding to AT.
ariaPatterns.offlineLiveRegion: Offline status banner uses role='status' aria-live='polite'.

name: app-workspace-switcher
role: Left-anchored workspace identity + switcher dropdown. Renders as a button that displays the current workspace logo + name; clicking opens a popover listing all workspaces the user belongs to + 'Create workspace' action. The popover is positioned absolutely below the trigger.

semanticStructure: <span class='logo'> — workspace logo (SVG or initial letter on accent ground)
<div role='menu' id='workspace-popover'> — popover content; not part of the trigger primitive but anchored to it
meta.popoverContent: List of workspaces (each with logo + name + plan badge + current-workspace ✓ marker) + divider + 'Create workspace' action + 'Workspace settings' link. Composes button-system action-button intent='ghost' for each row.

ariaPatterns.currentWorkspaceMarker: The current workspace in the popover gets aria-current='true' + visible ✓ indicator.
ariaPatterns.popoverDismiss: Escape closes + returns focus to trigger. Click outside closes.

name: app-breadcrumb
role: Middle path indicator showing the user's location within the workspace hierarchy. Renders as <nav aria-label='Breadcrumb'> containing <ol> of breadcrumb segments separated by visual chevrons. Each segment is a link except the last (current page, no link). Truncates middle segments with '…' when path is too deep.

meta.truncation: When path exceeds max-segments, render first + … + last 2 segments. The … is a clickable button that expands the full path in a popover.

ariaPatterns.currentPageMarker: Last segment uses <span aria-current='page'> (not <a>) so it's not a link.

name: app-global-search
role: Cmd+K / Ctrl+K command palette trigger. Renders as a search-input-shaped button with placeholder 'Search…' + Cmd+K keyboard hint pill. Clicking or pressing Cmd+K opens the full command palette (out of scope for v1 — host page provides). Trigger maintains aria-keyshortcuts + aria-expanded.

meta.platformAwareness: Render '⌘K' on Mac, 'Ctrl+K' on Windows / Linux via JS user-agent detection or CSS-only with prefers-platform queries (not yet standardised; default to ⌘K with Ctrl+K visible on Windows via JS).

ariaPatterns.popupRole: aria-haspopup='dialog' because the command palette is a modal dialog, not a menu.
ariaPatterns.ariaExpanded: Set aria-expanded='true' when the palette is open.

name: app-action-cluster
role: Right-of-search action buttons. Hosts page-specific actions (e.g. 'Share', 'New document', 'Filter', 'Export'). Composes <action-button> from pattern-button-system. Variable content per-page — this primitive defines the layout container, not the contents.

registers.compact: Up to 2 action buttons. Used in condensed chrome and on narrow viewports.
registers.default: 2-4 action buttons + optional 1 primary CTA at the rightmost slot.
registers.extended: 4-6 buttons; common on power-user pages (admin dashboards, large editor surfaces).

meta.composesFrom: @quoin/pattern-button-system — every action button is an <action-button> with appropriate intent (ghost for de-escalated actions, secondary for medium, primary for the highest-intent action like 'Share' or 'Publish').

ariaPatterns.groupLabel: <div role='group' aria-label='Page actions'> wraps the cluster.
ariaPatterns.iconOnlyActions: Icon-only buttons must have aria-label naming the action (e.g. aria-label='Filter results').

name: app-notification-bell
role: Bell-icon button showing unread notification count via badge. Click opens a popover listing recent notifications. Composes <action-button intent='ghost' size='md'> from pattern-button-system for the trigger; popover content is host-provided in v1.

meta.badgeDisplay: Show '99+' when unread > 99. Hide badge entirely when unread = 0.

ariaPatterns.ariaLabelCountAware: aria-label MUST include the unread count: 'Notifications, 3 unread' or 'Notifications, no unread'. The number in the visual badge is aria-hidden because the aria-label says it.
ariaPatterns.popoverPattern: Same as workspace-switcher and user-menu — popover dismiss via Escape + click-outside; focus returns to trigger.

name: app-user-menu
role: Rightmost avatar button that opens a user-account popover. The avatar can render initials, profile photo, or default silhouette. Popover hosts: profile link, account settings, theme toggle, keyboard-shortcuts cheatsheet, logout. Composes button-system for popover rows.

meta.avatarRendering: Priority: profile photo → initials (first letter of first + last name on --accent-recede ground) → silhouette icon. Photo / initials are square but rendered with border-radius: 999px (circle).
meta.popoverContent: Profile name + email header; ‘View profile’ / ‘Account settings’ / ‘Workspace settings’ / ‘Theme’ submenu / ‘Keyboard shortcuts’ / divider / ‘Log out’. Each row is a button-system action-button intent='ghost'.

ariaPatterns.ariaLabelIncludeName: aria-label MUST include the user's name so AT announces the account context.
ariaPatterns.statusDotSemantics: Status dot is aria-hidden; if presence is meaningful, expose it via a separate Online.

name: app-page-tabs
role: Secondary nav row below the main chrome. Hosts page-level tabs (Overview / Activity / Settings / Members). Rendered as <nav aria-label='Page sections'> with <ul> of tab links/buttons. Each tab maintains aria-current='page' for the active section.

registers.underline: Active tab has a 2px underline in --accent. The canonical Linear / GitHub / Notion register.
registers.pill: Active tab is a filled pill with --surface-elevated background. Used when underline conflicts with adjacent UI.
registers.boxed: Active tab is a connected box on top of the page content. Used for tabs that switch the entire page content (true 'tab panels').

ariaPatterns.navLabel: <nav aria-label='Page sections'> distinguishes from main nav-app-chrome navigation.
ariaPatterns.currentPageMarker: Active tab uses aria-current='page' (since each tab is a separate route, not a panel switcher within the same page).

name: app-status-banner
role: Top-anchored status banner. Renders above the main chrome row when the app is in a degraded state (offline, syncing-paused, maintenance-pending). Three tones — info (sync paused), warning (degraded service), critical (offline / data-loss risk).

ariaPatterns.liveRegion: <div role='status' aria-live='polite'> for info + warning. <div role='alert' aria-live='assertive'> for critical (offline, data-loss).
ariaPatterns.dismissPolicy: Info banners are dismissible (one-time announcements). Warning + critical are NOT dismissible — they reflect a live state that changes when the underlying condition clears.
